intent: A tclass=unix_stream_socket denial (haproxy_t denied connectto on an initrc_t-labeled socket). Exercises the socket class perm vocabulary (connectto from the unix_stream_socket class extension, not a file perm).
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). For decline scenarios these MAY disagree on purpose.

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: audit2allow -R substitutes the init_stream_connect_script() interface macro, which grants more than the single connectto perm (the interface bundles the socket connect path plus supporting accesses on initrc-script-related types). Our expected.te emits exactly `allow haproxy_t initrc_t:unix_stream_socket connectto;` - the one denied perm on the one triple, no macro, no extra types.
oracle.audit2why_note: audit2why reports 'Missing type enforcement (TE) allow rule' - a clean TeAllowable.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive parser that hard-codes file-class perms will not recognize connectto (a unix_stream_socket class-specific perm). The narrow fix must pass the socket perm through verbatim and target the unix_stream_socket class, not file.
